text.title: OpenAI Fine-Tuning Evaluation — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `fine-tuning-openai-eval` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Eval set MUST be held-out: hash-disjoint from train + val, never seen during fine-tune.
rule.rationale: Eval on data the model has seen overstates quality; production traffic exposes the gap.

rule.statement: ≥200 examples per eval set (≥500 preferred); below this the CI swamps the metric.
rule.rationale: Sample-size statistics: 200 examples give ~7% CI at 95% confidence for proportions.

rule.statement: Require ≥2 task-relevant metrics; one single metric is a leaky proxy.
rule.rationale: Optimising for a single metric gamifies the metric (Goodhart's Law).

rule.statement: LLM-as-judge prompt + rubric are version-controlled; never edited mid-eval.
rule.rationale: Editing the rubric mid-run invalidates comparison; always re-run fully on rubric change.

rule.statement: Report each metric with ±CI (Wilson interval for proportions, bootstrap for arbitrary metrics).
rule.rationale: Point estimates hide statistical ties; CI overlap = no significant improvement.

rule.statement: Gate decision is binary per metric (pass/fail); overall = all-pass.
rule.rationale: Implicit gates produce political deploys; explicit per-metric gates force evidence-based decisions.
